definition: Extension of Gopinath's dominant currency paradigm to cloud services. US ~65-70% of global hyperscale cloud revenue (~$350B, growing 20-25%). Structural current account inflows from recurring AI/cloud subscription revenue with high switching costs (data gravity, API lock-in). IMF COFER reserve diversification (~0.5-0.7pp/yr USD share decline) is a stock adjustment while technology invoicing flows represent growing private-sector demand.
evidence: US ~65-70% of global hyperscale cloud revenue (~$350B, growing 20-25%)
Switching costs from data gravity and API lock-in create stickiness
IMF COFER: ~0.5-0.7pp/yr USD share decline is stock adjustment
Partially corroborated by fx_strategist_02's dollar liquidity illusion framework
open_questions: Could European/Asian cloud alternatives gain sufficient share to erode the invoicing premium?
Is the technology invoicing flow structurally different from goods invoicing in terms of currency substitution risk?
Does the subscription model create more or less currency stickiness than transaction-based trade?
